mechanism: Inhibits RAF, VEGFR, PDGFR and FLT3, blocking proliferative signaling in AML cells
additional_notes: Strong activity against FLT3‑ITD AML including MOLM13; approved for renal cell carcinoma and hepatocellular carcinoma

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and other kinases, suppressing FLT3‑driven signaling
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; approved for FLT3‑mutated AML; low‑nanomolar activity in FLT3‑ITD cells including MOLM13

mechanism: Potent inhibitor of BCR‑ABL and FLT3, including resistant mutants, blocking oncogenic kinase signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML/ALL; strong preclinical activity in FLT3‑ITD AML lines including MOLM13

mechanism: Inhibits SRC family kinases, ABL, KIT, PDGFR and other tyrosine kinases, reducing downstream proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Approved for CML and Ph+ ALL; shows activity in some AML subtypes and FLT3‑ITD signaling pathways

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL, KIT, and PDGFR, blocking oncogenic signaling in kinase‑dependent cells
additional_notes: Originally for CML and GIST; limited efficacy in most AML but active in KIT‑dependent subsets; useful as control in MOLM13

mechanism: Second‑generation BCR‑ABL inhibitor with activity against KIT and PDGFR, blocking multiple oncogenic kinases
additional_notes: Approved for CML; broader kinase inhibition than imatinib; some activity in AML with KIT/PDGFR involvement

mechanism: Inhibits VEGFR, PDGFR, KIT, and FLT3, impairing receptor tyrosine kinase signaling and proliferation
additional_notes: Approved for renal cell carcinoma and GIST; reported to inhibit FLT3‑mutant AML cell proliferation

mechanism: Selectively binds and inhibits BCL‑2, promoting mitochondrial apoptosis in leukemia cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑199; approved for CLL and AML; MOLM13 is BCL‑2‑dependent and sensitive at low‑nanomolar levels

mechanism: Inhibits BCL‑2 and BCL‑XL, triggering apoptosis via mitochondrial pathway
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑263; tool compound for probing BCL‑2 family dependence in AML cell lines

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, leading to chromatin remodeling, altered gene expression, and apoptosis/differentiation
additional_notes: Also known as LBH589; approved for multiple myeloma; active in FLT3‑ITD AML models

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, increasing histone acetylation and inducing antiproliferative and pro‑apoptotic gene expression
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; approved for cutaneous T‑cell lymphoma; often combined with hypomethylating agents or FLT3 inhibitors in AML studies

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and inhibits DNA methyltransferases, causing DNA hypomethylation and induction of differentiation/apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as 5‑aza‑2′‑deoxycytidine; approved for MDS/AML; for 72‑h assays, fresh drug should be added every 24 h due to instability

mechanism: Nucleoside analog that inhibits DNA methyltransferases, leading to hypomethylation and altered gene expression
additional_notes: Approved for MDS/AML; may require drug replenishment in longer assays

mechanism: Binds FKBP12 and inhibits mTORC1, reducing protein synthesis and cell growth
additional_notes: Also known as Sirolimus; immunosuppressant; targets PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway frequently activated in AML including FLT3‑ITD

mechanism: Derivative of rapamycin that inhibits mTORC1 via FKBP12 binding, suppressing growth and proliferation
additional_notes: Also known as RAD001; approved for renal cell carcinoma, breast cancer, and NETs; shows AML growth inhibition in preclinical studies

mechanism: Activates AMPK and inhibits mitochondrial complex I, altering cellular energy metabolism and indirectly inhibiting mTOR signaling
additional_notes: Type 2 diabetes drug; exhibits antiproliferative and pro‑differentiation effects in leukemia; high‑micromolar to millimolar IC50 in vitro; monitor osmolarity at high doses

mechanism: Inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase and, in presence of copper, can inhibit NF‑κB and proteasome activity, inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Often tested with Cu²⁺ (e.g., CuCl₂ 0.5–1 µM); suggested 10 nM–10 µM alone, or 10 nM–1 µM with copper; targets ALDH‑positive leukemic stem/progenitor cells

mechanism: Antagonizes dopamine receptors and disrupts autophagy and calcium homeostasis, leading to selective AML cell death in some models
additional_notes: Reported to target leukemic stem cells and spare normal hematopoietic stem cells in some studies; watch for nonspecific toxicity at higher doses

mechanism: Binds tubulin and disrupts microtubule formation, inhibiting mitosis; may also affect hedgehog signaling
additional_notes: Repurposed anticancer candidate with antiproliferative effects in several cancers including leukemias

mechanism: Generates oxidative stress, induces apoptosis, and promotes degradation of oncogenic proteins
additional_notes: Also known as ATO; standard therapy for APL (PML‑RARA+); shows broader AML activity at higher concentrations
